master_row.title: Quantum Coherence and Decoherence
master_row.canonical_statement: This entry formalizes how quantum superpositions lose coherence; curvature and zero-point fluctuations accelerate decoherence.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as an expectation / trace over state relating V, r, C.
master_row.dictionary: Quantum coherence on the Trawinistic manifold is quantified by the field coherence metric (C(_1,_2)= _1,_2 _1 _2 ); decoherence occurs when (C 0) due to coupling with environmental degrees of freedom or curvature‑induced noise.
master_row.encyclopedia: Coherence is normalized state overlap; decoherence is the decay of that overlap under admissible disturbance. % CANONICAL PDF-ALIGNED VERSION Interpretive role: This entry gives the registry a clean coherence diagnostic. It also prepares the next steps in decoherence, pointer states, and measurement disturbance by giving a direct quantity that can decay under interaction.
